ffmpeg_encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual ffmpeg_encoder.h(3)

NAME

ffmpeg_encoder.h - FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char *output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t **out)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const uint8_t *rgb, int pitch, uint64_t captured_ns)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const float *samples, int num_samples)
void ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

Overview

The FFmpeg encoder provides a simple interface for encoding video frames or images to disk. The encoder automatically detects the output format and codec from the file extension, handling all necessary color space conversions and encoder configuration.

Input format: RGB24 (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per pixel) Output: File on disk with automatic format/codec detection

Supported Formats & Codecs

Extension Container Video Codec Audio Codec Pixel Format Use Case .mp4, .mov MP4 H.264 AAC YUV420P General streaming, web playback .mkv Matroska H.264 AAC YUV420P High-quality archival, flexible codecs .webm WebM VP9 Opus YUV420P Web-optimized streaming, best compression .avi AVI MPEG-4 PCM YUV420P Legacy format, older systems .flv Flash H.264 AAC YUV420P Flash video, streaming history .ogv, .ogg Ogg Theora Vorbis YUV420P Free/open-source streaming .3gp 3GP H.264 AAC YUV420P Mobile devices, low bitrate .gif GIF GIF None Palette Animated clips, social media .png Image PNG None RGB24 Lossless still images, single frame .jpg, .jpeg Image MJPEG None YUVJ420P Lossy still images, single frame

Default format: MP4 (if extension not recognized) Audio: Automatically selected based on container; image formats are audio-free

Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs

Automatic Bitrate Calculation:

  • Based on resolution: ~1 Mbps per megapixel
  • Range: 500 kbps (minimum) to 5000 kbps (maximum)
  • Formula: bitrate_kbps = (width × height) / 1024, clamped to [500, 5000]
  • Provides reasonable quality for most use cases without manual tuning

Color Space Conversion:

  • Input: RGB24 (24-bit color, standard in graphics/video capture)
  • Conversion method: libswscale with SWS_BICUBIC algorithm
  • SWS_BICUBIC: Bicubic interpolation offering good quality-to-speed balance
  • Pixel format selection:
•
YUV420P: Video formats (H.264, VP9, MPEG-4) — standard ITU-R BT.601 conversion
•
YUVJ420P: JPEG-based formats (MJPEG) — uses full-range YUV (0-255)
•
RGB24: Lossless formats (PNG, GIF) — preserves exact RGB values

Quality vs. Performance:

  • H.264: Best compression, moderate speed (suitable for streaming)
  • VP9: Better quality/size ratio, slower encoding (smaller files)
  • PNG: Lossless, larger files, very slow encoding (use for critical frames)
  • GIF: Lossless animation, severely limited palette (max 256 colors)
  • MJPEG: Fast single-frame capture, reasonable compression

Single-Frame Image Handling:

  • PNG and JPEG formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) output only one frame
  • Multiple ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame() calls write subsequent frames to the same file
  • Useful for snapshots or periodic frame capture

Usage Examples

Encode video to MP4 (default format):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.mp4", 1920, 1080, 30, &enc);
if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    // Handle error
}
// Write frames (RGB24 format, pitch in bytes per row)
uint8_t *frame_rgb = get_frame_data();
int pitch = 1920 * 3;  // 1920 pixels × 3 bytes per pixel
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame_rgb, pitch);
// Cleanup and finalize file
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

High-quality WebM encoding (better compression than H.264):

// Simply use .webm extension
ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.webm", 1920, 1080, 24, &enc);
// ... write frames ...
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

Capture single frame to PNG (lossless snapshot):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("snapshot.png", 1920, 1080, 1, &enc);
uint8_t *frame = capture_frame();
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 1920 * 3);
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes and closes file

Animated GIF from frame sequence:

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("animation.gif", 640, 480, 10, &enc);  // 10 FPS
for (int i = 0; i < num_frames; i++) {


    uint8_t *frame = get_frame(i);


    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 640 * 3);
}
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes GIF with palette

Common Pitfalls & Best Practices

1. Pitch/Stride Parameter:

  • pitch is the byte offset from one row to the next
  • For tightly-packed RGB: pitch = width × 3
  • For aligned/padded buffers: use actual row size (may be larger)
  • Must match the actual memory layout of the input buffer

2. Memory Management:

  • Input frame data is NOT owned by encoder — caller must manage
  • Output file is owned by encoder and written/closed by ffmpeg_encoder_destroy()
  • All resources freed in ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() — always call it

3. Error Recovery:

  • Encoder is NOT usable after any error from ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame()
  • Must call ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() to clean up and finalize file
  • If file operations fail, file may be partially written

4. File Extension Sensitivity:

  • Extension matching is case-sensitive on Unix-like systems
  • Use lowercase extensions (.mp4 not .MP4) for maximum compatibility
  • Unknown extension defaults to MP4 H.264

5. Platform Support:

  • This encoder is NOT AVAILABLE on Windows (_WIN32)
  • Only POSIX systems (Linux, macOS) are supported

Performance Considerations

Encoding Speed (relative):

  • Fastest: GIF, MJPEG (lossless single-frame)
  • Fast: H.264 with default bitrate
  • Medium: VP9 (better compression at cost of speed)
  • Slow: PNG (lossless color preservation)

File Size (for 1080p @ 30fps, 10 seconds):

  • MP4 H.264: ~50-100 MB (depends on bitrate)
  • WebM VP9: ~30-60 MB (better compression)
  • AVI MPEG-4: ~100-150 MB (less efficient compression)
  • PNG: ~100+ MB per frame (lossless, single frame only)
  • GIF: ~200+ MB for 300 frames (palette limited)

Bitrate Impact on Quality:

  • Default bitrate is calculated for acceptable quality at various resolutions
  • For critical content (e.g., document sharing), consider PNG for single frames
  • For streaming, MP4 H.264 offers best balance of quality, compatibility, and size
  • VP9 recommended for highest quality requirements when file size isn't critical

Definition in file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Definition at line 163 of file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char * output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t ** out)

Create and initialize a video/image encoder.

The output format and codec are automatically determined from the file extension. See Supported Formats & Codecs for supported formats.

Bitrate: Automatically calculated from resolution (~1 Mbps per megapixel, clamped to 500-5000 kbps).

Color Space: Input is expected in RGB24 format. Internal conversion to the appropriate output pixel format (YUV420P for video, RGB24 for PNG, YUVJ420P for JPEG) is handled automatically.

Parameters

output_path Path to the output file (extension determines format and codec)
width_px Video width in pixels (must be > 0)
height_px Video height in pixels (must be > 0)
fps Frames per second (must be > 0)
out Pointer to encoder handle (set on success)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The encoder acquires resources (file handles, memory buffers) that must be released with ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(). Failure to do so may leak file handles and memory.

See also

ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame, ffmpeg_encoder_destroy, ffmpeg_encoder_formats

Definition at line 337 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.

338                                                                 {
339   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] START: output=%s, %dx%d @ %dfps", output_path, width_px, height_px, fps);
340 
341   if (!output_path || !out || width_px <= 0 || height_px <= 0)
342     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_create: invalid parameters");
343 
344   // Use default FPS if not specified (0 from config means "use default")
345   if (fps <= 0) {
346     fps = 60;
347     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: fps was 0, using default 60");
348   }
349 
350   ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*enc), ffmpeg_encoder_t *);
351   enc->width_px = width_px;
352   enc->height_px = height_px;
353 
354   // For snapshot mode, calculate output FPS based on input FPS and snapshot duration
355   // If we capture for N seconds at input_fps, we'll get ~(N * input_fps) frames
356   // Output duration should be snapshot_delay seconds
357   // So output_fps = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
358   // EXCEPT: if the input was probed, fps parameter is the actual input fps
359   // Then output_fps should equal input_fps to make frame durations sum correctly
360   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
361   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
362   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
363             snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
364 
365   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
366     // For snapshot mode: if we capture at input_fps for snapshot_delay seconds,
367     // we get approximately (input_fps * snapshot_delay) frames
368     // To make output span exactly snapshot_delay seconds:
369     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
370     // So we should use input_fps as output_fps!
371     // Example: 30fps input for 3s → ~90 frames → output at 30fps → 90/30 = 3 seconds ✓
372 
373     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
374              snapshot_delay, fps);
375     log_info("  → Will estimate ~%.0f frames, output FPS=%d makes durations sum to %.1f seconds",
376              fps * snapshot_delay, fps, snapshot_delay);
377   }
378 
379   enc->fps = fps;
380 
381   // Snapshot mode frame distribution setup
382   enc->estimated_frame_count = 0;
383   enc->estimated_frame_duration = 0;
384   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0 && fps > 0) {
385     // Estimate how many frames we'll capture
386     enc->estimated_frame_count = (int)(fps * snapshot_delay + 0.5);
387     // Each frame should have uniform duration in stream time_base (1/90000 for MP4)
388     // frame_duration = total_duration / estimated_frame_count
389     //                = snapshot_delay seconds * time_base.den / estimated_frame_count
390     // We'll set this to stream->time_base later after it's initialized
391     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode ENABLED - estimated_frame_count=%d (fps=%d * snapshot_delay=%.2f)",
392               enc->estimated_frame_count, fps, snapshot_delay);
393   } else {
394     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode disabled or invalid - snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.2f, fps=%d",
395              snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
396   }
397   enc->is_stdout_pipe = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) ? 1 : 0;
398 
399   // For codec/format detection, use output_path unless it's "-" (stdout)
400   // For stdout, use "fake.mp4" to default to MP4 format
401   const char *detection_path = output_path;
402   if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
403     detection_path = "fake.mp4"; // Fake path for format detection when piping to stdout
404     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using MP4 format for stdout piping");
405   }
406 
407   const char *codec_name = NULL, *format_name = NULL;
408   get_codec_from_extension(detection_path, &codec_name, &format_name, &enc->is_image, &enc->target_pix_fmt);
409 
410   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: %s (%s, %dx%d @ %dfps, %s)", output_path, codec_name, width_px, height_px, fps,
411             enc->is_image ? "image" : "video");
412 
413   // Allocate output media context
414   int ret = avformat_alloc_output_context2(&enc->fmt_ctx, NULL, format_name, output_path);
415   if (ret < 0) {
416     SAFE_FREE(enc);
417     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_alloc_output_context2 failed");
418   }
419 
420   // Find encoder
421   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name(codec_name);
422   if (!codec) {
423     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
424     SAFE_FREE(enc);
425     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: encoder '%s' not found", codec_name);
426   }
427 
428   // Create new stream
429   enc->stream = avformat_new_stream(enc->fmt_ctx, codec);
430   if (!enc->stream) {
431     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
432     SAFE_FREE(enc);
433     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_new_stream failed");
434   }
435 
436   // Allocate codec context
437   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
438   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
439     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
440     SAFE_FREE(enc);
441     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_alloc_context3 failed");
442   }
443 
444   // Set codec parameters
445   enc->codec_ctx->width = width_px;
446   enc->codec_ctx->height = height_px;
447   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = enc->target_pix_fmt;
448   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, fps};
449   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){fps, 1};
450 
451   // Set bitrate for high quality (music-level quality)
452   // Use ~5-10 Mbps per megapixel for visually lossless quality
453   int bitrate = (width_px * height_px * 10) / 1024; // higher quality estimate in kbps
454   if (bitrate < 4000)
455     bitrate = 4000; // Minimum 4000 kbps for quality
456   if (bitrate > 50000)
457     bitrate = 50000; // Cap at 50 Mbps max
458   enc->codec_ctx->bit_rate = bitrate * 1000;
459   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: bitrate set to %d kbps for %dx%d", bitrate, width_px, height_px);
460 
461   // Configure codec-specific options for quality
462   AVDictionary *codec_opts = NULL;
463   if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx264") == 0) {
464     // x264 fast settings for real-time rendering (optimize for speed over quality)
465     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0); // ultrafast preset for real-time encoding
466     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "28", 0); // Lower quality (crf 28 = reasonable quality, much faster)
467     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x264 preset=ultrafast with crf=28 for real-time encoding");
468   }
469 
470   // Open codec (capture libx264 startup logs)
471   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
472     ret = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, &codec_opts);
473   });
474   av_dict_free(&codec_opts);
475   if (ret < 0) {
476     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
477     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
478     SAFE_FREE(enc);
479     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_open2 failed");
480   }
481 
482   // Copy codec parameters to stream
483   ret = avcodec_parameters_from_context(enc->stream->codecpar, enc->codec_ctx);
484   if (ret < 0) {
485     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
486     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
487     SAFE_FREE(enc);
488     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_parameters_from_context failed");
489   }
490 
491   // Muxer options for proper MP4 container structure
492   AVDictionary *opts = NULL;
493 
494   // Muxer flags configuration
495   // For stdout/non-seekable output, use fragmented MP4 (frag_keyframe)
496   // For regular files, use faststart (moov at front for streaming)
497   bool is_stdout = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0);
498   if (is_stdout) {
499     // Use fragmented MP4 for stdout - starts a new fragment at each video keyframe
500     // This allows streaming without requiring file seeking (FFmpeg docs recommended)
501     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "+frag_keyframe", 0);
502     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using fragmented MP4 (+frag_keyframe) for stdout output");
503   } else {
504     // Move moov atom to the front (faststart) - helps with streaming and player compatibility
505     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "faststart", 0);
506   }
507 
508   if (enc->is_image) {
509     // Use -update flag for single frame output
510     av_dict_set(&opts, "update", "1", 0);
511   }
512 
513   // Allocate frames
514   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
515   enc->frame_encoded = av_frame_alloc();
516   enc->pkt = av_packet_alloc();
517   if (!enc->frame || !enc->frame_encoded || !enc->pkt) {
518     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
519     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
520     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
521     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
522     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
523     SAFE_FREE(enc);
524     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: frame allocation failed");
525   }
526 
527   // Set frame properties for target pixel format
528   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
529   enc->frame_encoded->width = width_px;
530   enc->frame_encoded->height = height_px;
531   ret = av_frame_get_buffer(enc->frame_encoded, 32);
532   if (ret < 0) {
533     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
534     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
535     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
536     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
537     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
538     SAFE_FREE(enc);
539     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: av_frame_get_buffer failed");
540   }
541 
542   // Create SWS context for RGB → target format conversion
543   LOG_IO("swscaler", {
544     enc->sws_ctx = sws_getContext(width_px, height_px, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, width_px, height_px, enc->target_pix_fmt,
545                                   SWS_BICUBIC, NULL, NULL, NULL);
546   });
547   if (!enc->sws_ctx) {
548     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
549     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
550     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
551     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
552     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
553     SAFE_FREE(enc);
554     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: sws_getContext failed");
555   }
556 
557   // Initialize audio stream if supported
558   encoder_init_audio_stream(enc, output_path);
559 
560   // Open output file or stdout
561   if (!(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE)) {
562     // For stdout output, use "pipe:1" (FFmpeg's standard for writing to stdout)
563     const char *open_path = output_path;
564     if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
565       open_path = "pipe:1"; // Use pipe:1 for stdout
566       log_debug("ffmpeg: Redirecting '-' to 'pipe:1' for stdout output");
567     }
568     ret = avio_open(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb, open_path, AVIO_FLAG_WRITE);
569     if (ret < 0) {
570       sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
571       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
572       av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
573       av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
574       avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
575       avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
576       SAFE_FREE(enc);
577       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avio_open failed for '%s'", open_path);
578     }
579   }
580 
581   // Write header (capture FFmpeg format muxer logs)
582   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
583     ret = avformat_write_header(enc->fmt_ctx, &opts);
584   });
585   av_dict_free(&opts); // Free options after use
586   if (ret < 0) {
587     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
588     sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
589     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
590     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
591     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
592     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
593     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
594     SAFE_FREE(enc);
595     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_write_header failed");
596   }
597 
598   // Now that stream->time_base is set, calculate estimated frame duration for snapshot mode
599   bool snapshot_mode_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
600   double snapshot_delay_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
601   if (snapshot_mode_after_header && enc->estimated_frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay_after_header > 0) {
602     // Each frame should span: snapshot_delay / estimated_frame_count seconds
603     // In stream time_base units: (snapshot_delay * time_base.den) / estimated_frame_count
604     enc->estimated_frame_duration =
605         (int64_t)((snapshot_delay_after_header * (double)enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->estimated_frame_count);
606     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Calculated estimated_frame_duration=%lld time_base units "
607               "for snapshot mode (snapshot_delay=%.2f, estimated_frames=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
608               (long long)enc->estimated_frame_duration, snapshot_delay_after_header, enc->estimated_frame_count,
609               enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den);
610   }
611 
612   enc->frame_count = 0;
613   enc->video_pts = 0;
614   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = 0.0;
615   enc->snapshot_elapsed_ns = 0;
616   enc->first_frame_captured_ns = 0;  // Initialize first frame timestamp
617   *out = enc;
618 
619   /* Register encoder with named registry */
620   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER(enc, output_path, NULL);
621 
622   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] SUCCESS: encoder initialized, frame_count=0, fps=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->fps, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
623   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
624 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_image,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe, log_debug, log_info,
    LOG_IO, NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SET_ERRNO, ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_elapsed_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc)


Close and finalize the output file, releasing all encoder
    resources.




This function flushes any pending frames in the encoder, writes
    the file trailer, and releases all allocated resources (memory buffers, file
    handles, codecs).




Critical: This MUST be called to properly finalize the
    output file. Without this call, the file may be incomplete or
  unplayable.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (currently always succeeds)


Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (returns ASCIICHAT_OK).



After this call, enc must not be used again.




The output file is NOT guaranteed to be complete until this
    function returns.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create


Definition at line 876 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


876                                                                 {
877   if (!enc)
878     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
879 
880   NAMED_UNREGISTER(enc);
881 
882   // In snapshot mode, calculate the FPS needed to match the target duration
883   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
884   int64_t adjusted_packet_duration = 0;
885   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
886 
887   if (snapshot_mode && enc->frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay > 0) {
888     // Calculate output FPS that distributes frames across snapshot_delay seconds
889     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay
890     // packet_duration (in stream time_base) = stream_time_base.den / output_fps
891     //                                        = (stream_time_base.den * snapshot_delay) / frame_count
892     adjusted_packet_duration = (int64_t)((snapshot_delay * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
893     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - adjusting packet durations for %d frames over %.2f seconds",
894              enc->frame_count, snapshot_delay);
895     log_debug("  Adjusted packet duration: %lld units (%.6f seconds per frame)",
896               (long long)adjusted_packet_duration,
897               (double)adjusted_packet_duration / enc->stream->time_base.den);
898   }
899 
900   // Flush video encoder (capture libx264 final statistics)
901   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
902     avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, NULL);
903     int flush_pkt_count = 0;
904     while (1) {
905       int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
906       if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
907         break;
908       if (ret < 0)
909         break;
910 
911       flush_pkt_count++;
912 
913       // Set packet duration in codec time base if not already set
914       if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
915         enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
916       }
917 
918       // In snapshot mode, rescale to stream time_base and apply adjusted duration if set
919       int64_t duration_before = enc->pkt->duration;
920       av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
921       if (snapshot_mode && adjusted_packet_duration > 0) {
922         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_packet_duration;
923         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flush packet %d duration adjusted from %lld to %lld",
924                   flush_pkt_count, (long long)duration_before, (long long)adjusted_packet_duration);
925       }
926       enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
927       av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
928       av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
929     }
930   });
931 
932   // Flush audio encoder if present
933   if (enc->has_audio_stream && enc->audio_codec_ctx) {
934     // Write any remaining partial audio samples as padding
935     if (enc->audio_partial_len > 0) {
936       // Zero-pad the remaining samples
937       memset(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], 0,
938              (enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len) * sizeof(float));
939       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
940       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
941       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
942       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
943       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
944     }
945 
946     // Flush any remaining packets (capture FFmpeg audio codec logs)
947     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
948       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, NULL);
949       while (1) {
950         int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
951         if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
952           break;
953         if (ret < 0)
954           break;
955 
956         av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
957         enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
958         av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
959         av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
960       }
961     });
962   }
963 
964   // Set stream duration for proper metadata (must be before trailer)
965   if (enc->stream && enc->frame_count > 0) {
966     int64_t duration;
967     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
968     if (snapshot_mode) {
969       extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
970       double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
971       // Use actual capture elapsed time if available, otherwise use snapshot_delay
972       // This ensures frames are distributed across the correct time window
973       double actual_capture_duration = g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
974       double effective_duration = (actual_capture_duration > 0) ? actual_capture_duration : snapshot_delay;
975 
976       // In snapshot mode with wall-clock timing, the video duration should equal the actual elapsed time
977       // duration_seconds = effective_duration
978       // duration_time_base_units = effective_duration * time_base.den
979       duration = (int64_t)(effective_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den);
980 
981       // Calculate what the frame durations should be to sum to effective_duration
982       // This ensures FFmpeg plays the captured frames across the full effective_duration
983       // effective_frame_duration = effective_duration / frame_count (seconds per frame)
984       int64_t effective_frame_duration = (int64_t)((effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count) * enc->stream->time_base.den);
985 
986       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - snapshot_delay=%.2f, frames=%d, output_duration=%.2f sec",
987                snapshot_delay, enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
988       log_debug("  Using effective_duration=%.2f seconds for output", effective_duration);
989       log_debug("  stream->time_base=%d/%d, effective frame duration=%lld time_base units",
990                 enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den, (long long)effective_frame_duration);
991       log_debug("  (each frame should span %.3f seconds to total %.2f seconds)",
992                 effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
993 
994       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Setting stream->duration=%lld (effective_duration=%.3f, time_base=%d/%d, equiv=%.3f sec)",
995                (long long)duration, effective_duration,
996                enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den,
997                (double)duration * enc->stream->time_base.num / enc->stream->time_base.den);
998     } else {
999       // Normal mode: calculate duration from frame count and FPS
1000       // time_base = 1 / time_base.den seconds per unit
1001       // Each frame is 1/fps seconds, so duration = frame_count / fps seconds
1002       // In time base units: duration = (frame_count / fps) * time_base.den = frame_count * time_base.den / fps
1003       duration = (int64_t)enc->frame_count * enc->stream->time_base.den / enc->fps;
1004       log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Normal mode - Set stream duration=%lld (frames=%d, fps=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
1005                 (long long)duration, enc->frame_count, enc->fps, 1, enc->stream->time_base.den);
1006     }
1007     enc->stream->duration = duration;
1008 
1009     // CRITICAL: Adjust last frame duration to ensure total duration matches stream->duration
1010     // Each frame was encoded with FPS-based duration, but snapshot mode needs them distributed
1011     // across the actual capture duration. Since we can't change past frame durations, we extend
1012     // the last frame's duration to make the sum equal the desired total.
1013     if (snapshot_mode) {
1014       // Calculate sum of all frame durations (each frame currently has the same FPS-based duration)
1015       int64_t current_total_duration = enc->video_pts;  // This is the sum of all frame durations so far
1016       int64_t desired_duration = duration;
1017 
1018       // The last frame needs to be extended to: desired - (sum of all other frames)
1019       // This ensures the video plays for exactly the snapshot_delay duration
1020       int64_t last_frame_extension = desired_duration - current_total_duration;
1021 
1022       if (last_frame_extension > 0) {
1023         // We need to extend the last frame. But we can't modify it directly after encoding.
1024         // Instead, the muxer will use stream->duration as the authoritative source.
1025         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Last frame would need extension of %lld units to reach total %lld (currently %lld)",
1026                   (long long)last_frame_extension, (long long)desired_duration, (long long)current_total_duration);
1027       }
1028     }
1029   }
1030 
1031   // Write trailer (capture FFmpeg muxer logs and final frame statistics)
1032   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1033     av_write_trailer(enc->fmt_ctx);
1034   });
1035 
1036   // Flush output buffer for stdout/pipes before closing
1037   if (enc->fmt_ctx && enc->fmt_ctx->pb) {
1038     avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1039     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flushed output buffer");
1040   }
1041 
1042   // Close output file
1043   if (enc->fmt_ctx && !(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE))
1044     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1045 
1046   // Cleanup
1047   sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
1048   av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
1049   av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
1050 
1051   // Cleanup audio resources (capture any remaining FFmpeg logs from codec cleanup)
1052   if (enc->has_audio_stream) {
1053     swr_free(&enc->audio_swr_ctx);
1054     av_frame_free(&enc->audio_frame);
1055     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1056       avcodec_free_context(&enc->audio_codec_ctx);
1057     });
1058     SAFE_FREE(enc->audio_partial_buf);
1059   }
1060 
1061   av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
1062   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1063     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
1064   });
1065   if (enc->fmt_ctx)
1066     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
1067 
1068   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: wrote %d frames", enc->frame_count);
1069   SAFE_FREE(enc);
1070   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1071 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_swr_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_debug,
    log_info, LOG_IO, NAMED_UNREGISTER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_FREE,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts.


Referenced by render_file_destroy().






void
  ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc,
  double actual_duration_sec)


Set the actual wall-clock duration for snapshot mode frame
  timing.




In snapshot mode, frames are captured over a wall-clock duration
    (e.g., 3 seconds of real time). This function should be called after all
    frames are captured to provide the actual elapsed time, allowing the encoder
    to calculate correct frame durations so the output video duration matches
    the actual capture time.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


actual_duration_sec Actual wall-clock duration in seconds (e.g., 3.5 for
  3.5 seconds)


Note


Only used in snapshot mode. In normal mode, this is
  ignored.



Should be called before ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() for the
    adjustment to take effect.




If not called, frame durations are calculated from FPS, which may
    not match actual wall-clock capture time.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 857 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


857                                                                                                     {
858   if (!enc)
859     return;
860   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = actual_duration_sec;
861 
862   // If we already have frame count, we can calculate the adjusted packet duration now
863   // This will be used for any remaining frames and during flush
864   if (enc->frame_count > 0 && actual_duration_sec > 0) {
865     // Calculate the packet duration that would make all frames fit in actual_duration_sec
866     // packet_duration_units = (actual_duration_sec * time_base.den) / frame_count
867     // This is only a starting point - the actual duration might be different if more frames are captured
868     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: %.3f sec with %d frames so far",
869               actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
870   } else {
871     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: Setting duration to %.3f seconds (already encoded %d frames)",
872             actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
873   }
874 }






References ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count, log_debug,
    log_info, and ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration.


Referenced by
  render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const float * samples, int num_samples)


Write audio samples to the output file.




Encodes and writes audio samples to the output file. Audio samples
    must be in 48kHz mono float32 format. The function internally buffers and
    accumulates samples into codec frames before encoding.




Audio Format Requirements:


    
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz (48 kHz)
    • 
  
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
    • 
  
  • Format: 32-bit IEEE float (AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT)
    • 



Notes:


    
  
  • Audio is only written if the output format supports audio (MP4, WebM,
      AVI).
    • 
  
  • Image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF) ignore audio writes.
    • 
  
  • Samples are buffered internally and encoded in chunks as needed.
    • 
  
  • Timing is automatically synchronized with video using PTS (presentation
      timestamp).
    • 



Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


samples Pointer to audio samples in float32 format (not owned, used
  immediately)


num_samples Number of samples to write (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input sample data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



Safe to call on encoders without audio streams (no-op).




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame


Definition at line 790 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


790                                                                                                            {
791   if (!enc || !samples || num_samples <= 0)
792     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Silently ignore invalid calls
793 
794   // No audio stream for this format
795   if (!enc->has_audio_stream)
796     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
797 
798   // Accumulate samples into partial buffer
799   int samples_to_process = num_samples;
800   int samples_offset = 0;
801 
802   while (samples_to_process > 0) {
803     // How many samples can we add to the partial buffer?
804     int space_in_buffer = enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len;
805     int samples_to_copy = (samples_to_process < space_in_buffer) ? samples_to_process : space_in_buffer;
806 
807     // Copy samples to partial buffer
808     memcpy(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], &samples[samples_offset], samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
809     enc->audio_partial_len += samples_to_copy;
810     samples_offset += samples_to_copy;
811     samples_to_process -= samples_to_copy;
812 
813     // If buffer is full, encode and write frame
814     if (enc->audio_partial_len >= enc->audio_frame_size) {
815       // Set up audio frame with accumulated samples
816       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
817       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
818       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
819       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
820 
821       // Send frame to encoder
822       int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
823       if (ret < 0) {
824         log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame for audio failed");
825       } else {
826         // Receive and write packets
827         while (ret >= 0) {
828           ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
829           if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
830             break;
831           if (ret < 0) {
832             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet for audio failed");
833             break;
834           }
835 
836           // Rescale timestamp and write
837           av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
838           enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
839           ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
840           av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
841           if (ret < 0) {
842             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame for audio failed");
843             break;
844           }
845         }
846       }
847 
848       // Update PTS for next frame
849       enc->audio_pts += enc->audio_frame_size;
850       enc->audio_partial_len = 0;
851     }
852   }
853 
854   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
855 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_warn_every, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const uint8_t * rgb, int pitch,
  uint64_t captured_ns)


Write a single video frame to the output file.




The frame must be in RGB24 format (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per
    pixel). Internal color space conversion is performed automatically based on
    the output format (see Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs for
    details).




Pitch Parameter: The pitch parameter specifies the byte
    offset from one row to the next. For tightly-packed RGB data: pitch =
    width_px × 3. For padded/aligned buffers: pitch must reflect the
    actual row size.




Timestamp Parameter: The captured_ns parameter is the
    wall-clock timestamp (in nanoseconds) when the frame was captured. This
    ensures video playback uses actual capture timing, not encoder processing
    time. For correct frame timing in the output video, captured_ns should be
    the time when the frame was acquired from the media source.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


rgb Pointer to frame data in RGB24 format (not copied, used immediately)


pitch Byte offset between rows (must match actual buffer layout)


captured_ns Wall-clock timestamp in nanoseconds when frame was
  captured


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input frame data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



For image-only formats (PNG, JPEG), only the last frame written is
    kept. Previous frames are discarded when the next frame is written.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 626 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


627                                                                    {
628   if (!enc || !rgb)
629     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: NULL input");
630 
631   // Initialize first frame capture time on first call
632   if (enc->first_frame_captured_ns == 0) {
633     enc->first_frame_captured_ns = captured_ns;
634     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: first frame captured at %llu ns", (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
635   }
636 
637   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_WRITE] frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
638   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d, captured_ns=%llu", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px,
639             enc->height_px, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
640 
641   // Set up RGB frame for conversion
642   uint8_t *data[1] = {(uint8_t *)rgb};
643   int linesize[1] = {pitch};
644 
645   // CRITICAL: Ensure frame format is set before each sws_scale call
646   // Some FFmpeg versions reset the format field, so we need to set it explicitly
647   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
648 
649   // Convert RGB24 to target format
650   sws_scale(enc->sws_ctx, (const uint8_t *const *)data, linesize, 0, enc->height_px, enc->frame_encoded->data,
651             enc->frame_encoded->linesize);
652 
653   // Set frame duration based on mode
654   int64_t frame_duration;
655   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
656 
657   if (enc->frame_count == 0) {
658     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame 0, snapshot_mode=%d", snapshot_mode);
659   }
660 
661   // Use FPS-based frame duration (snapshot mode adjusts total duration in destroy())
662   frame_duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
663 
664   enc->frame_encoded->duration = frame_duration;
665 
666   // Track this frame's capture timestamp for duration calculation of next frame
667   if (snapshot_mode) {
668     uint64_t frame_dur_ms = (enc->previous_captured_ns > 0) ? (captured_ns - enc->previous_captured_ns) / 1000000 : 0;
669     if (enc->frame_count <= 2) {
670       log_info("ffmpeg: frame %d SET duration=%lld units (%.3fms since last, codec_base=1/%d)",
671                enc->frame_count, (long long)frame_duration, (double)frame_dur_ms,
672                enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den);
673     }
674     enc->previous_captured_ns = captured_ns;
675   }
676 
677   // Calculate PTS from actual capture timestamp
678   // time_base is {1, fps}, so 1 PTS unit = 1/fps seconds
679   // Convert elapsed nanoseconds to PTS units: elapsed_ns * fps / 1_000_000_000
680   uint64_t elapsed_ns = captured_ns - enc->first_frame_captured_ns;
681   int64_t pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)((elapsed_ns * (uint64_t)enc->fps) / 1000000000ULL);
682 
683   // In snapshot mode, distribute frames linearly across the actual duration
684   if (snapshot_mode) {
685     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
686     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
687     if (g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms > 0) {
688       // Distribute frames linearly based on their capture timestamp relative to total duration
689       // pts = (frame_elapsed_ns / total_elapsed_ns) * actual_duration_sec * fps
690       // This ensures frame 0 is at 0s and last frame is at actual_duration_sec
691       double actual_duration_sec = (double)g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
692       double total_elapsed_sec = (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
693 
694       if (total_elapsed_sec > 0.001) {
695         double frame_fraction = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
696         double target_pts_sec = frame_fraction * actual_duration_sec;
697         int64_t pts_before = pts_from_timestamp;
698         // Convert target seconds to time_base units: pts = target_sec * time_base.den / time_base.num
699         pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)(target_pts_sec * (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num);
700 
701         if (enc->frame_count < 3 || enc->frame_count % 10 == 0) {
702           log_debug("ffmpeg: snapshot frame %d: PTS distributed (before=%lld, after=%lld, sec=%lld->%lld, frac=%.3f)",
703                     enc->frame_count, (long long)pts_before, (long long)pts_from_timestamp,
704                     (long long)((double)pts_before * enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num / enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den),
705                     (long long)target_pts_sec, frame_fraction);
706         }
707       }
708     }
709   }
710 
711   // Use timestamp-based (or scaled) PTS
712   enc->frame_encoded->pts = pts_from_timestamp;
713   enc->video_pts += frame_duration;
714 
715   if (!snapshot_mode) {
716     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: PTS calculation - elapsed_ns=%llu, pts_from_timestamp=%lld, fps=%d",
717               (unsigned long long)elapsed_ns, (long long)pts_from_timestamp, enc->fps);
718   }
719 
720   // Send frame to encoder
721   int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, enc->frame_encoded);
722   if (ret < 0) {
723     log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame failed");
724     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Continue anyway
725   }
726 
727   // Receive packets from encoder and write to file
728   static int write_frame_pkt_count = 0;
729 
730   while (ret >= 0) {
731     ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
732     if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
733       break;
734     if (ret < 0) {
735       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet failed");
736       break;
737     }
738 
739     write_frame_pkt_count++;
740 
741     // Set packet duration in codec time base (duration of one frame at target FPS)
742     // This ensures proper stream duration calculation
743     if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
744       enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
745     }
746 
747     av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
748     enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
749 
750     // In snapshot mode, apply adjusted duration if snapshot_actual_duration is set
751     // This happens when we get the actual duration from the capture thread
752     bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
753     if (is_snapshot_mode && enc->snapshot_actual_duration > 0 && enc->frame_count > 0) {
754       int64_t adjusted_dur = (int64_t)((enc->snapshot_actual_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
755       // Only apply if it's significantly different from the default
756       if (adjusted_dur > enc->pkt->duration * 2) {
757         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_dur;
758         log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame packet %d duration adjusted to %lld units based on actual duration",
759                   write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration);
760       }
761     } else if (!is_snapshot_mode && write_frame_pkt_count < 3) {
762       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d, duration=%lld, snapshot_mode=%d, actual_dur=%.2f, frame_count=%d",
763                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration, is_snapshot_mode, enc->snapshot_actual_duration,
764                 enc->frame_count);
765     }
766 
767     if (write_frame_pkt_count <= 2 || write_frame_pkt_count >= 16) {
768       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d with final duration=%lld (%.6f sec in stream time_base), total frames=%d",
769                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration,
770                 (double)enc->pkt->duration / enc->stream->time_base.den, enc->frame_count);
771     }
772 
773     ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
774     av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
775     if (ret < 0) {
776       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame failed");
777       break;
778     }
779 
780     // Flush output buffer for stdout to ensure frames are written immediately
781     if (enc->is_stdout_pipe) {
782       avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
783     }
784   }
785 
786   enc->frame_count++;
787   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
788 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms,
    g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn_every,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::previous_captured_ns, SET_ERRNO,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().

    Version 0.9.4
